translations.text: And after they join themselves unto him, he worketh deceit, and hath increased, and hath been strong by a few of the nation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Peaceably even into the fertile places of the province He cometh, and he hath done that which his fathers did not, nor his fathers' fathers; prey, and spoil, and substance, to them he scattereth, and against fenced places he deviseth his devices, even for a time.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he stirreth up his power and his heart against the king of the south with a great force, and the king of the south stirreth himself up to battle with a very great and mighty force, and standeth not, for they devise devices against him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those eating his portion of food destroy him, and his force overfloweth, and fallen have many wounded.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And both of the kings' hearts [are] to do evil, and at one table they speak lies, and it doth not prosper, for yet the end [is] at a time appointed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he turneth back [to] his land with great substance, and his heart [is] against the holy covenant, and he hath wrought, and turned back to his land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At the appointed time he turneth back, and hath come against the south, and it is not as the former, and as the latter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And ships of Chittim have come in against him, and he hath been pained, and hath turned back, and hath been insolent toward the holy covenant, and hath wrought, and turned back, and he understandeth concerning those forsaking the holy covenant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And strong ones out of him stand up, and have polluted the sanctuary, the stronghold, and have turned aside the continual [sacrifice], and appointed the desolating abomination.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those acting wickedly [against] the covenant, he defileth by flatteries; and the people knowing their God are strong, and have wrought.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the teachers of the people give understanding to many; and they have stumbled by sword, and by flame, by captivity, and by spoil -- days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in their stumbling, they are helped -- a little help, and joined to them have been many with flatteries.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And some of the teachers do stumble for refining by them, and for purifying, and for making white -- till the end of the time, for [it is] yet for a time appointed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the king hath done according to his will, and exalteth himself, and magnifieth himself against every god, and against the God of gods he speaketh wonderful things, and hath prospered till the indignation hath been completed, for that which is determined hath been done.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And unto the God of his fathers he doth not attend, nor to the desire of women, yea, to any god he doth not attend, for against all he magnifieth himself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to the god of strongholds, on his station, he giveth honour; yea, to a god whom his fathers knew not he giveth honour, with gold, and with silver, and with precious stone, and with desirable things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath dealt in the fortresses of the strongholds with a strange god whom he hath acknowledged; he multiplieth honour, and hath caused them to rule over many, and the ground he apportioneth at a price.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And at the time of the end, push himself forward with him doth a king of the south, and storm against him doth a king of the north, with chariot, and with horsemen, and with many ships; and he hath come in to the lands, and hath overflowed, and passed over,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hath come into the desirable land, and many do stumble, and these escape from his hand: Edom, and Moab, and the chief of the sons of Ammon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he sendeth forth his hand upon the lands, and the land of Egypt is not for an escape;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he hath ruled over treasures of gold and of silver, and over all the desirable things of Egypt, and Lubim and Cushim [are] at his steps.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And reports trouble him out of the east and out of the north, and he hath gone forth in great fury to destroy, and to devote many to destruction;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he planteth the tents of his palace between the seas and the holy desirable mountain, and hath come unto his end, and there is no helper to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And at that time stand up doth Michael, the great head, who is standing up for the sons of thy people, and there hath been a time of distress, such as hath not been since there hath been a nation till that time, and at that time do thy people escape, every one who is found written in the book.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the multitude of those sleeping in the dust of the ground do awake, some to life age-during, and some to reproaches -- to abhorrence age-during.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those teaching do shine as the brightness of the expanse, and those justifying the multitude as stars to the age and for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou, O Daniel, hide the things, and seal the book till the time of the end, many do go to and fro, and knowledge is multiplied.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have looked -- I, Daniel -- and lo, two others are standing, one here at the edge of the flood, and one there at the edge of the flood,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to the one clothed in linen, who [is] upon the waters of the flood, 'Till when [is] the end of these wonders?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I hear the one clothed in linen, who [is] upon the waters of the flood, and he doth lift up his right hand and his left unto the heavens, and sweareth by Him who is living to the age, that, 'After a time, times, and a half, and at the completion of the scattering of the power of the holy people, finished are all these.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have heard, and I do not understand, and I say, 'O my lord, what [is] the latter end of these?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith, 'Go, Daniel; for hidden and sealed [are] the things till the time of the end;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Purify themselves, yea, make themselves white, yea, refined are many: and the wicked have done wickedly, and none of the wicked understand, and those acting wisely do understand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and from the time of the turning aside of the perpetual [sacrifice], and to the giving out of the desolating abomination, [are] days a thousand, two hundred, and ninety.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the blessedness of him who is waiting earnestly, and doth come to the days, a thousand, three hundred, thirty and five.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou, go on to the end, then thou dost rest, and dost stand in thy lot at the end of the days.'
